title: coussarea meridionalis
intro: Coussarea meridionalis, a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, is also known as the "Meridional Coussarea" or "West Indian Coussarea". This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Coussarea meridionalis include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. Herbal preparations of Coussarea meridionalis typically involve the leaves and bark of the plant, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
